title: TAIH Symposium 2026
text:
About the Symposium
Machine learning and artificial intelligence are increasingly deployed in high-stakes domains. However, ensuring trustworthiness, transparency, and accountability remains a critical challenge. This symposium aims to advance research by exploring robust methodologies, interpreting model-driven decisions, and ensuring reliability in real-world use cases.

title: TAIH Symposium 2026
text:
Confirmed Speakers
The symposium includes a number of talks by invited speakers. More details will follow soon.



Jessalyn Holodinsky, PhD
Assistant Professor
University of Calgary


View Bio


Dr. Jessalyn Holodinsky is an Assistant Professor of Data Science in the Department of Emergency Medicine at the University of Calgary, where she leads the Calgary Emergency Medicine Data Lab and is the Director of AI and Data Education through the Office of Faculty Development at the Cumming School of Medicine. Her work explores how AI can be used to predict patient flow, anticipate demand, and optimize how emergency departments operate. She is particularly interested in how AI systems behave in the real world: not just whether they work in theory, but whether they work in the right settings, for the right populations, and in ways that meaningfully improve how care is delivered. At the core of her work is a simple belief: that better data, used well, saves lives.





Henry Leung, PhD
Professor
University of Calgary


View Bio


Dr. Henry Leung is a Professor in the Department of Electrical and Software Engineering. His research focuses on machine learning, data analytics, information fusion, robotics, sensor networks and IoT, and signal and image processing.

title: Sponsorship Opportunities
text: The Transdisciplinary Artificial Intelligence Hub (TAIH) is proud to partner with organizations committed to the future of transparent and impactful AI. Our sponsors play a vital role in bringing this inaugural symposium to the University of Calgary.

title: Sponsorship Opportunities
text:
Partnering with TAIH
We are actively seeking academic and corporate partners for the TAIH Symposium 2026. Sponsoring our event provides an excellent opportunity to showcase your organization to leading researchers and top-tier students at the University of Calgary.

Why Sponsor?

Visibility: Gain exposure among leading AI and technology practitioners.
Recruitment: Network directly with top graduate students and postdocs.
Impact: Support the growth of our transdisciplinary AI academic community.

title: Call for Papers
text: We invite submissions for the TAIH Symposium 2026. We welcome contributions from researchers and practitioners across academia and industry who are exploring the intersection of artificial intelligence and domain-specific challenges.

title: Call for Papers
text:
Important Dates

Submission deadline: May 5, 2026 11:59 PM MDT
Author notification: May 17, 2026, 11:59 PM MDT
Camera-ready deadline: May 22, 2026, 11:59 PM MDT

title: Call for Papers
text:
Topics of Interest
We welcome a broad spectrum of research on transdisciplinary efforts in development and use of AI. Examples are:

AI in healthcare and medicine
AI in critical infrastructure and energy
Human-AI interaction and collaboration
Ethical, governance, and societal implications of AI
Transdisciplinary case studies and real-world applications
Trustworthy ML foundations (explainability, interpretability, fairness, robustness, etc.)

title: Call for Papers
text:
Submission Guidelines
We use a flexible, one-page format to make joining the conversation easy for all researchers.

What to Submit

Length: 1 page, plus references.
Format: Flexible. You may submit an extended abstract, preliminary results, or a summary of recently published or previously presented work.
Template: Standard single-column or double-column academic format (e.g., IEEE, ACM, or standard LaTeX article). PDF format is required.
Review Process: All submissions will be peer-reviewed in a double-blind process. Please ensure your submitted PDF does not contain author names or affiliations.



Presentation Format
Upon acceptance, authors will be invited to present their research at the symposium’s Poster Session.

The physical poster should reflect the core concepts of the submitted abstract, but authors are encouraged to include any additional details, figures, and updates.
Top Submissions: The authors of the top three accepted papers will be invited to present their work during the main plenary session, in addition to their poster.


Submit on OpenReview
